package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for goroutine_captures_loop_variable: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `for _, v := range items { go func() { use(v) }() }` without rebinding `v` inside the loop body (pre-Go 1.22)
type CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableModel
}

type CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableService struct {
    repo *CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveGoroutineCapturesLoopVariable(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGoroutineCapturesLoopVariableDTO) error {
    focus := "goroutine_captures_loop_variable"
    _ = focus
    shared := map[string]string{}
    go func() { shared[input] = input }()
    shared[input+"x"] = input
    goroutine_captures_loop_variable_positive := map[string]int{"goroutine": 1, "captures": 2, "loop": 3, "variable": 4}
    goroutine_captures_loop_variable_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    goroutine_positive_0 := ctx
    _ = goroutine_positive_0
    captures_positive_1 := goroutine_captures_loop_variable_positive["captures"] + len(input)
    _ = captures_positive_1
    for loop_positive_2Index, item := range items { goroutine_captures_loop_variable_positive[item.ID] = loop_positive_2Index }
    variable_positive_3 := goroutine_captures_loop_variable_positive["variable"] + len(input)
    _ = variable_positive_3
    return nil
}
